REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK, NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE, OCWEN LOAN SERVICES, LLC, AND WESTERN PROGRESSIVE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ADDRESS IN ITS OPPOSITION (1) LACK OF STANDING TO CHALLENGE THE SECURITIZATION RE: CAUSES OF ACTION 1-4 AND 7; (2) HBOR RE: CAUSES OF ACTION 6 AND 8; AND (3) LACK OF ALLEGATIONS REGARDING ANY PROMISES MADE BY THE MOVING DEFENDANTS RE: CAUSE OF ACTION 5. ORDER IS SIGNED APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER, KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS (415-948-8289) CSR #12998, REPORTED. (D501)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Continued To Mar-11-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Estavillo, Esq. (510-982-3001) for Plaintiff/opposing party Raymond Parker-Gaylord.
Thomas Abbott, Esq. of Severson & Werson (415-398-3344) for Defendant/moving party Oewen, Western Progressive and Deutsche Bank.
Court reporter, Karla Ellis-Davis (415-948-8289) CSR #12998.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK, NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE, OCWEN LOAN SERVICES, LLC, and WESTERN PROGRESSIVE, LLC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to address in its opposition (1) lack of standing to challenge the securitization re: causes of action 1-4 and 7; (2) HBOR re: causes of action 6 and 8; and (3) lack of allegations regarding any promises made by the moving defendants re: cause of action 5. Order is signed approved as to form. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter, Karla Ellis-Davis (415-948-8289) CSR #12998, Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Continued To Jan-28-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2014 Continued To Jan-22-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Estavillo, Esq. of Law Offices of Jason Estavillo, (510-982-3001), APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL for Raymond Parker-Gaylord, Plaintiff and opposing party.
No other appearance.

Mr. Estavillo, counsel for plaintiff, complied with the Rule 3.1308 and requests another date of hearing. Court grants the request and rules as follows: DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK, NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE, OCWEN LOAN SERVICES, LLC, WESTERN PROGRESSIVE, LLC DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to January 22, 2015. The Court is in receipt of courtesy copy of reply. Reply does not appear to have been filed with the Court. Defendant shall address this issue and submit a declaration with explanation no later than January 1, 2014. Plaintiff's counsel to give notice to the other party. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Lynn O. Taylor; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Jason W. Estavillo, Esq. (510-982-3001), attorney for plaintiff/opposing party
Tina Naicker, Esq. of Bryan Cave LLP (415-675-3400), attonrey for defendant JPMorgan Chase, defendant/demurring party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY and JPMORGAN CHASE BANK's AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to Cause of Action 5, promissory estoppel. Plaintiff's complaint makes out a valid claim for promissory estoppel and factual issues exist with regard to the nature of Defense Counsel's promise and his ability to bind the foreclosing party that preclude sustaining the demurrer. The demurrer is SUSTAINED without leave to amend on all other causes of action. Defendants were not the foreclosing parties, and Plaintiff has not alleged prejudice stemming from his allegations that the wrong party is foreclosing on the debt he admits he owes. Furthermore, the Homeowner's Bill of Rights is not retroactive and therefore does not apply to this case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
